source_policy.authority: Official OpenAI Agents SDK documentation plus installed package version; local code comments are not source of truth when docs differ.
refresh_required_before: enabling SDK sessions, handoffs, tracing, tool guardrails, MCP, or sandbox agents
blocked_sources: Chinese-origin model/provider/tooling docs and packages are out of scope by trust policy

official_sources.title: OpenAI Agents SDK
local_relevance: Agents SDK is production-ready Python package for agentic apps.
Core primitives include agents, handoffs/agents-as-tools, guardrails, tracing, sessions, MCP, and sandbox agents.
Responses API and Agents SDK can coexist; SDK is best for managed workflows.
implementation_bindings: plugins/openai_agents/tools.py uses Agent and Runner.run_sync for bounded lanes.
Hermes remains the top-level orchestrator; SDK workers are subordinate lanes.

official_sources.title: Agents
local_relevance: Agent supports instructions, tools, handoffs, guardrails, structured outputs, lifecycle hooks, and tool-use behavior.
Agent plus Runner provides orchestration for turns, tools, guardrails, handoffs, and sessions.
implementation_bindings: GovernedAgentOutput is used as output_type for structured proof.
Current implementation intentionally does not enable SDK tools, sessions, or handoffs yet.

official_sources.title: Results
local_relevance: Runner.run_sync returns RunResult with final_output, new_items, raw_responses, and replay/state surfaces.
final_output may be typed output, string, or None depending on run state.
implementation_bindings: _extract_usage reads raw_responses for best-effort token usage.

official_sources.title: Guardrails
local_relevance: Blocking input guardrails run before expensive model execution and prevent token/tool spend on tripwire.
Output guardrails validate final agent output; tool guardrails are needed for every custom tool call in delegated workflows.
implementation_bindings: _build_agent_guardrail_kwargs uses run_in_parallel=False for input scope guardrails.
SDK tool guardrails are future work because SDK workers intentionally lack side-effect tools today.

local_assumptions.claim: Hermes owns session/memory orchestration; SDK sessions are disabled until isolated multi-turn SDK workflows are explicitly designed.
local_assumptions.recheck_trigger: Before adding SDK sessions or handoffs.

local_assumptions.claim: SDK tracing remains disabled and sensitive trace data excluded until a sanitized opt-in trace processor is designed.
local_assumptions.recheck_trigger: Before enabling OpenAI tracing or dashboards.

local_assumptions.claim: SDK workers do not inherit Hermes terminal/file/browser/memory tools; Hermes orchestrator handles side effects.
local_assumptions.recheck_trigger: Before adding SDK function tools, MCP tools, or sandbox agents.
